name: Bunch o' Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine 1/2 cup flour with yeast, cardamom, and nutmeg.
2. Heat the apple juice, maple syrup, and melted butter to body temperature; ensure it is not above 100°F (38°C); add to the yeast mixture.
3. Stir in orange liqueur.
4. Beat on low speed until mixed, then increase speed and fold in the remaining flour until incorporated.
5. Turn onto a floured work surface and knead for at least 10 minutes.
6. Place dough in an oiled bowl; turn so the top is also coated. Let rise for 1 hour.
7. Punch down and let rest for 10 minutes.
8. Remove 1/4 of the dough; set aside.
9. Shape the remaining dough into 2-inch balls.
10. Arrange on an oiled cookie sheet in the shape of a bunch of grapes.
11. Brush with egg white and sprinkle with poppy seeds.
12. With the remaining dough, form grape vine and leaves; attach to the grapes at the top.
13. Brush leaves and stem with egg white.
14. Let rise for 30 minutes in a warm place.
15. Melt butter and honey together; slash grape leaves and brush slashes with the mixture.
16. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 20-30 minutes, or until the bread makes a thunking sound when tapped with a knuckle.
